text: The surgeon uses robotic instruments to mobilize the splenic flexure down to the level of the low sigmoid colon. Dense pelvic adhesions are visible, likely resulting from a previous hysterectomy.

The surgeon uses a monopolar device to perform dissection both laterally and anteriorly. The distal sigmoid colon is visible, fistulized to the vagina. The dissection continues to separate the vagina from the sigmoid colon and divide the fistula.
The surgeon continues lateral and posterior dissection down to the upper rectum. EEA instruments are placed in the rectum and vagina to facilitate retraction during the procedure.

The surgeon safely identifies the ureter before using a vessel sealer device to take the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and rectosigmoid mesentery. The peritoneal reflection of the proximal rectum is incised with a monopolar device.

The surgeon removes a left ovarian cyst for improved exposure, pedicolizing the left ovary and securing the ovarian artery with a clip and vessel sealer device. The cyst is placed into a specimen extraction bag for removal with the specimen at the end of the procedure.

The surgeon further dissects the rectum and divides the mesorectum up to the bowel wall. A blue load robotic stapler is used with three firings to transect the rectum. The divided colovaginal fistula is visible in the surgical field.

ICG angiography is used to identify the transition between perfused and purply perfused colon, which is marked with cautery. The specimen is externalized, and the diseased bowel is transected. EEA sizers assist in selecting the twenty-nine millimeter stapler, with the anvil placed and delivered back into the abdomen. The anastomosis is performed laparoscopically.

The surgeon completes the anastomosis using the EEA stapler, then removes the device. A bowel clamp is placed proximal to the anastomosis site. Proctoscopy is performed, and the leak test shows no leakage.
A 19 French Blake drain is positioned near the anastomosis and exits through the left lateral port site, secured in place.
